info.name: CampusPath AI - AgentCore
info.description:
# CampusPath AI AgentCore API

AgentCore exposes a single HTTP endpoint: `POST /invocations`.
CampusPath routes work by sending a required `route` field in the JSON body.

## Allowed routes

- `placement-doubt`
- `career-roadmap`
- `resume-review`
- `interview-prep`

You may also send `action` instead of `route`. Invalid or missing values return a validation error with `allowed_routes`.

## Shared model controls

Optional on every request. Defaults are applied when omitted:

| Field | Default |
| --- | --- |
| `model_id` | `global.amazon.nova-2-lite-v1:0` |
| `temperature` | `0.4` |
| `top_p` | `0.9` |
| `max_tokens` | `2048` |

## Response contract

Successful responses include:

- `route` and `route_title`
- `answer`
- `model_params` (values actually used)
- `usage` (`input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `total_tokens`)
- `cost` (approximate USD estimate)
- `latency_ms`

## Start AgentCore

```bash
source campuspath-venv/bin/activate
cd CampusAgent
agentcore dev --no-browser
```

request.description: Checks whether the local AgentCore runtime is running.

item.name: Validation examples

request.description: Omits `route` / `action`. Expect `ok: false` and a validation error listing allowed routes.
Uses an unsupported route. Expect a validation error.

item.description: Shows how CampusPath rejects missing or unknown routes.

item.name: Campus placement workflows

request.description:
Route: `placement-doubt`

Required:
- `route` (or `action`)
- `prompt` OR `question`

Optional:
- `student_profile`
- `model_id`, `temperature`, `top_p`, `max_tokens`

Response includes answer, model_params used, Bedrock token usage, approximate cost, and latency.

body.raw:
{
  "route": "career-roadmap",
  "goal": "Cloud / DevOps Engineer",
  "degree": "B.Tech IT",
  "year": "3rd year",
  "skills": "Python, Git, Linux basics, introductory AWS",
  "hours_per_week": 10,
  "model_id": "{{modelId}}",
  "temperature": {{temperature}},
  "top_p": {{topP}},
  "max_tokens": {{maxTokens}}
}

request.description:
Route: `career-roadmap`

Required:
- `route`
- `prompt` OR `goal`

Optional structured fields: `degree`, `year`, `skills`, `hours_per_week` plus model controls.

Route: `resume-review`

Required:
- `route`
- `prompt` OR `resume_text`

Optional: `target_role` and model controls. Avoid sensitive personal data in demos.

body.raw:
{
  "route": "interview-prep",
  "role": "Backend Developer",
  "interview_type": "Mixed",
  "experience_level": "Beginner",
  "focus_topics": "Python, REST APIs, SQL, database design, college project discussion",
  "model_id": "{{modelId}}",
  "temperature": {{temperature}},
  "top_p": {{topP}},
  "max_tokens": {{maxTokens}}
}

request.description:
Route: `interview-prep`

Required:
- `route`
- `prompt` OR `role`

Optional: `interview_type`, `experience_level`, `focus_topics`, plus model controls.
Omits model controls so the agent applies defaults. Response still echoes the resolved `model_params`.

item.description: All workflows hit `POST /invocations` and are selected with the body `route` field.

item.name: Payload format examples

request.description: `action` is accepted as an alias for `route`.
Uses Bedrock-style `messages[]` content blocks with a required `route`.

item.description: Extra payload shapes supported by the CampusPath AgentCore entrypoint.
